title: Apache Kafka — LdapLoginModule not blocked in default JAAS denylist
query_goal: Detect cases where the default JAAS login-module denylist does not include LdapLoginModule, allowing an attacker who controls JAAS config to force an outbound LDAP connection to an arbitrary server.

steps.name: Locate the denylist default-value string literal
steps.description: Find every string literal that is used as the second argument (defaultValue) of a System.getProperty() call whose first argument is the disallowed-login-modules config key ('org.apache.kafka.disallowed.login.modules'). This literal is the fallback denylist when no system property has been set.
steps.codeql_sketch:
from MethodAccess ma, StringLiteral defaultVal
where ma.getMethod().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "System", "getProperty")
  and ma.getArgument(0).(StringLiteral).getValue() = "org.apache.kafka.disallowed.login.modules"
  and defaultVal = ma.getArgument(1)
select defaultVal, defaultVal.getValue()

steps.name: Check completeness of the denylist default value
steps.description: For each default-value string found in step 1, verify whether it contains the string 'LdapLoginModule'. A default value that omits this token is incomplete and constitutes the vulnerable condition. Also handle the case where the default value is held in a static-final field rather than an inline literal (trace through field reads).

steps.name: Identify the login-module name source (untrusted input)
steps.description: Find every call to AppConfigurationEntry.getLoginModuleName(). The return value is the class name supplied by whoever controls the JAAS configuration file or the sasl.jaas.config property — it is attacker-controllable.
steps.codeql_sketch:
from MethodAccess getModuleName
where getModuleName.getMethod().hasQualifiedName(
        "javax.security.auth.login", "AppConfigurationEntry", "getLoginModuleName")
select getModuleName

steps.name: Find the security-check sink (denylist membership test)
steps.description: Identify the point where the login-module name is tested against the denylist. Typically this is a Set<String>.contains(loginModuleName) call that follows the split/trim pipeline applied to the System.getProperty result. This is the sink: if the denylist reaching this check is incomplete, the check is ineffective.
steps.codeql_sketch:
from MethodAccess containsCall
where containsCall.getMethod().hasQualifiedName("java.util", "Set", "contains")
  // argument is the (trimmed) login module name
select containsCall

steps.name: Build a local data-flow / taint path from denylist construction to the contains() check
steps.description: Trace how the System.getProperty() return value flows into the Set that is tested at the sink. The pipeline is: getProperty → split(",") → stream().map(trim) → collect(toSet()) → set.contains(loginModuleName). Confirm that the Set used in the contains() call was built from the incomplete default string identified in step 2.

steps.name: Confirm that the login-module name reaches the same contains() check (source → sink taint)
steps.description: Using a taint-tracking configuration, verify that the result of getLoginModuleName() (step 3) reaches the argument of the contains() call (step 4). Combined with step 5, this confirms the full vulnerable path: untrusted module name is compared to an incomplete denylist.

steps.name: Compose final query and emit alert
steps.description: Combine all previous predicates: a denylist default string that is missing LdapLoginModule (steps 1-2), a login-module name source (step 3), an incomplete denylist flowing to the security-check Set (step 5), and the module name flowing to the contains() argument (step 6). Emit an alert with the location of the incomplete default-value literal.
steps.codeql_sketch:
from StringLiteral incompleteDenylist, MethodAccess containsCheck
where incompleteDenylistFlowsTo(incompleteDenylist, containsCheck)
  and moduleNameReaches(containsCheck)
  and not incompleteDenylist.getValue().matches("%LdapLoginModule%")
select incompleteDenylist,
  "Default denylist '" + incompleteDenylist.getValue() +
  "' does not block LdapLoginModule; an attacker-controlled JAAS config can use it to reach an arbitrary LDAP server."
